questions.question: A concave mirror is used as a shaving mirror because it:
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors produce magnified and erect images when the object is close to the mirror.

questions.question: The critical angle is defined as the angle of incidence in the denser medium at which:
questions.explanation: The critical angle is when the refracted ray in the rarer medium makes 90° with the normal.

questions.question: A convex mirror always produces:
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always produce virtual, erect, and smaller images, regardless of object position.

questions.question: A ray of light passes from air to glass. The angle of incidence is 30° and angle of refraction is 20°. The refractive index of glass is:
questions.explanation: Refractive index = sin(i)/sin(r) = sin30°/sin20° ≈ 1.5

questions.question: A ray incident at 45° on a plane mirror will reflect at an angle of:
questions.explanation: Angle of reflection equals angle of incidence, so 45°.

questions.question: If the refractive index of water is 1.33, the speed of light in water is approximately:
questions.explanation: Speed in medium = c/n = 3 × 10^8 / 1.33 ≈ 2.25 × 10^8 m/s

questions.question: A ray strikes a concave mirror at its pole. The reflected ray:

options.B: Reflects back along the incident path
options.D: Goes to the center of curvature

questions.explanation: A ray hitting the pole reflects symmetrically back along the same path.

questions.question: A ray of light traveling from glass (n=1.5) to air strikes the surface at an angle greater than critical angle. The ray will:
questions.explanation: Total internal reflection occurs when incidence > critical angle; light reflects back into glass.

questions.question: Which type of mirror is used as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors provide wide field of view and erect images.

questions.question: A ray passes from air into a medium and bends towards the normal. This indicates that:

options.A: Medium is less dense than air

questions.explanation: Bending towards normal indicates light enters a denser medium.

questions.question: For a concave mirror, when the object is at infinity, the image is formed at:
questions.explanation: Rays from a distant object converge at the focus of concave mirror.

questions.question: A ray parallel to principal axis after reflection from a concave mirror passes through:
questions.explanation: Parallel rays reflect and pass through focus in concave mirror.

questions.question: If a ray of light travels from air to water, the ratio of sine of angle of incidence to sine of angle of refraction is:
questions.explanation: Refractive index n = sin i / sin r = 1.33 for water.

questions.question: A diver sees the Sun vertically above in water. Due to refraction, the Sun appears:
questions.explanation: Objects appear higher due to light bending towards normal in denser medium.

questions.question: The distance between the pole and focus of a spherical mirror is called:
questions.explanation: Focal length is the distance between pole and focus of mirror.

questions.question: A ray of light enters a glass prism and deviates. This deviation is due to:
questions.explanation: Deviation occurs because light bends while entering and leaving prism (refraction).

questions.question: Total internal reflection is possible only when light travels from:
questions.explanation: TIR occurs when light moves from optically denser to rarer medium and incidence > critical angle.

questions.question: Which mirror can produce a real, inverted, and magnified image of an object placed between focus and center of curvature?
questions.explanation: Concave mirror forms real, inverted, magnified image when object is between focus and center of curvature.

questions.question: A ray strikes the surface of water at 48° and reflects at 48°. If it refracts at 32°, the refractive index of water is:
questions.explanation: n = sin i / sin r = sin48° / sin32° ≈ 1.33

questions.question: The virtual image formed by a plane mirror is always:
questions.explanation: Plane mirror forms virtual image of the same size as the object.

questions.question: In which medium does light travel the slowest among air, water, and glass?
questions.explanation: Light travels slowest in glass due to its higher refractive index.

questions.question: A real image is always formed on a screen when:
questions.explanation: Concave mirrors can form real images on a screen if the object is beyond focus.

questions.question: A pencil appears bent when partially immersed in water. This is due to:
questions.explanation: The apparent bending is due to refraction at water-air interface.

questions.question: A light ray traveling in air strikes a glass slab at 45° and emerges parallel to its original path. The lateral displacement occurs due to:
questions.explanation: Refraction at two surfaces of the slab causes lateral shift of the emergent ray.

questions.question: A ray incident along the principal axis of a concave mirror reflects:
questions.explanation: A ray incident along principal axis passes through pole and reflects along the same path.
